<h1 align="center">best-codex</h1>

<p align="center">
  <em>A focused workspace of small, practical tools for better Codex workflows.</em>
</p>

<p align="center">
  <img src="https://img.shields.io/badge/projects-4-111111?style=flat-square" alt="Projects: 4">

  <sub><a href="./README.md">English</a> &middot; <a href="./README.ko.md">한국어</a></sub>

---

<p align="center">
  <img src="./public/assets/readme/best-codex-hero.png" alt="best-codex hero banner" width="100%">
</p>

`best-codex` collects tools that make Codex easier to use across local
development, ChatGPT handoff, account switching, image generation, and
long-running agent sessions. Each project is intentionally standalone: install
and run the tool you need from its own directory.

> These projects are companion tools for local Codex and ChatGPT workflows.
> Some features use private or unofficial Codex/ChatGPT paths and may change
> without notice.

## Projects

| Project | Purpose | Runtime |
|---|---|---|
| [`codex-chatgpt-bridge`](./codex-chatgpt-bridge/) | Local MCP bridge for ChatGPT Developer Mode, bounded repo tools, and terminal-owned Codex handoff. | TypeScript, Bun/Node |
| [`codex-linker`](./codex-linker/) | Switch multiple Codex ChatGPT OAuth accounts while sharing one local setup. | TypeScript, Bun/Node |
| [`codex-status-bar`](./codex-status-bar/) | Tiny macOS menu bar app that shows live Codex CLI status. | Swift, Node hooks |
| [`codex-imagegen-api`](./codex-imagegen-api/) | Image generation API, CLI, and local HTTP server backed by the user's Codex ChatGPT login. | TypeScript, Bun |

## Quick Start

Clone the workspace and enter the project you want to use:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/bytonylee/best-codex.git
cd best-codex
```

Most TypeScript tools use the same local setup pattern:

```bash
cd codex-imagegen-api
bun install
bun run build
bun link
```

The macOS menu bar app builds from its own directory:

```bash
cd codex-status-bar
./build.sh
open -gj build/CodexStatusBar.app
```

## Tool Summaries

### codex-chatgpt-bridge

`codex-chatgpt-bridge` exposes a small MCP tool surface so ChatGPT Developer
Mode can inspect and change one local repository. ChatGPT can read, search,
edit, run bounded verification commands, save image artifacts, and write a
handoff plan. Codex execution stays in the terminal through
`execute-handoff` or `watch-handoff`.

```bash
cd codex-chatgpt-bridge
bun install
bun run build
bun link
cc-bridge start --no-auth
```

### codex-linker

`codex-linker` keeps `auth.json` separate per Codex account while symlinking
shared config, MCP files, skills, hooks, sessions, and history from the
primary Codex home. It does not proxy traffic or print tokens.

```bash
cd codex-linker
bun install
bun run build
bun link
codex-linker setup --accounts 3
```

### codex-status-bar

`codex-status-bar` is a macOS menu bar app for long Codex turns. Hooks write
local state under `~/.codex/statusbar/`, and the Swift app polls it to show
thinking, tool use, permission waits, and completion.

```bash
cd codex-status-bar
./build.sh
open -gj build/CodexStatusBar.app
```

### codex-imagegen-api

`codex-imagegen-api` provides one image generation core across CLI, library
API, and a local HTTP server. It supports prompt, image count, aspect ratio,
reference images, generated output layout, and provider fallback rules.

```bash
cd codex-imagegen-api
bun install
bun run build
bun link
imagegen --auth
```

## Security Notes

- Workspace tools are designed for local use and explicit user-controlled
  setup.
- Bridge and server tools bind locally by default and use token auth where
  remote or HTTP access is involved.
- Auth files and access tokens are not printed by the tools.
- Generated files and state are written into documented local directories.
- Unofficial private Codex/ChatGPT endpoints are opt-in or clearly called out
  in the individual project README files.

## Tests

Run checks from each project directory:

```bash
cd codex-chatgpt-bridge && bun test
cd codex-linker && bun test
cd codex-imagegen-api && bun test
```

For the status bar:

```bash
cd codex-status-bar
swiftc -O -target arm64-apple-macos12.0 Sources/*.swift tests/*Tests.swift -o /tmp/test -framework Cocoa
node tests/hook-state-contract.test.js
node tests/hook-install-contract.test.js
node tests/hook-lifecycle-contract.test.js
```
